---
title: "Optimization"
---

Optimization is the process of finding the maximum or minimum value of a 
function in the context of an applied problem. In economics, this translates 
directly to questions every firm and consumer faces: How much should a firm 
produce to maximize profit? At what price is revenue maximized? How does a 
consumer allocate a fixed budget to maximize utility? The calculus tools 
developed in the previous chapter give us a precise, systematic way to 
answer these questions.

## Key Functions in Economic Optimization

### Cost Functions

### Revenue Functions

### Profit Functions

## Marginal Analysis

### Marginal Cost

### Marginal Revenue

### Marginal Profit

## Profit Maximization

## The Role of Constraints

*To be continued... optimization under constraints and an introduction to Lagrangian methods.*
